message:
The other day, I found myself in a classic dog-owner moment: I was out for a walk with my pup, Bella, and I realized I had packed enough supplies to survive a week in the wilderness! Treats, water, poop bags, a first-aid kit, and even a squeaky toy (you know, for emergencies). As I watched Bella enthusiastically sniffing every bush, I couldn’t help but chuckle at my gloriously over-prepared self. 

But then it hit me – being prepared doesn’t just mean having the essentials; it’s about peace of mind. Knowing I have everything I need for Bella makes our adventures all the more enjoyable. That little refresher I came across recently helped me refine my doggie emergency kit, too! 

How about you? Are you more of a minimalist or an over-packager when it comes to your furry friends?
